sections.heading: Clinical Note
sections.section_value:
Chief Complaint
Left hand pain
History of Present Illness
The patient reports pain in the left hand, which began approximately two weeks ago. The pain is constant and is described as a dull ache. The patient denies any recent trauma. He reports that the pain is exacerbated by gripping objects and is slightly relieved by rest. He denies any numbness or tingling. He is currently taking ibuprofen as needed for the pain.
Findings
Physical exam reveals mild tenderness to palpation over the distal volar aspect of the left hand. Range of motion is limited due to pain. There is no swelling or erythema. Neurological exam is intact, with full strength and sensation in the hand and fingers.
Assessment
The patient's symptoms are most consistent with a superficial volar wrist tendinitis.
Plan
Prescribe ibuprofen 600mg every 6 hours as needed for pain. Instruct patient to avoid activities that aggravate the pain. Refer to physical therapy for evaluation and treatment. Schedule follow-up in 2 weeks to assess response to treatment.
